text.title: gce-managed-instance-groups Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for gce-managed-instance-groups as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting initial_delay_sec shorter than the actual VM startup and application warmup time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The autoheal loop will mark a healthy but still-starting VM as unhealthy, delete it, and recreate it. If all VMs are simultaneously starting (e.g., after a rolling restart), this creates a thrashing loop that can take the entire MIG offline until the initial delay is corrected.

failure-mode.description: Using OPPORTUNISTIC update policy for rolling updates in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OPPORTUNISTIC only replaces instances when they are recreated for other reasons (e.g., preemption, autohealing). The MIG can remain in a mixed-template state indefinitely. PROACTIVE replaces all instances on a schedule within the maxUnavailable/maxSurge constraints.
